title: Modulate cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: higher modulate depth should drive more visible frame-to-frame warp on the decoded fixture
the stronger modulate setting should materially move the exported fixture spectrum rather than leaving the audio static

expectedVideo: video fixture should remain decodable while UV warp deepens on non-procedural content
higher modulate depth should distort motion coherently, not freeze or explode the frame
expectedAudio: fixture audio should pick up phase-warp motion without dropping out or clipping
the cross-source case should still track the same depth law heard on osc
manualChecks: Does modulate remain coupled and usable on decoded media, not only oscillator content?
At the stronger setting, does the output still read as intentional rather than unstable?
